Apple___Apple_scab.symptoms: Olive-green to brown velvety spots on leaves and fruit. Leaves may curl and fall early. Infected fruit develops scabby, cracked lesions.
Apple___Apple_scab.pesticide: Mancozeb 75% WP (2.5g/L), Captan 50% WP (2g/L), or Myclobutanil 10% WP (1g/L)
precautions: Prune trees for better air circulation

Apple___Black_rot.symptoms: Brown, rotting lesions on fruit that turn black. Purple spots with brown centers on leaves (frog-eye leaf spot). Cankers appear on branches.
Apple___Black_rot.pesticide: Captan 50% WP (2g/L), Thiophanate-methyl 70% WP (1.5g/L), or Mancozeb 75% WP (2.5g/L)
precautions: Remove mummified fruit from trees and ground
Prune out dead or cankered wood
Apply fungicide during petal fall and every 10-14 days

Apple___Cedar_apple_rust.symptoms: Bright orange-yellow spots on upper leaf surfaces. Tube-like structures on undersides of leaves. Premature defoliation in severe cases.
Apple___Cedar_apple_rust.pesticide: Myclobutanil 10% WP (1g/L), Triadimefon 25% WP (1g/L), or Mancozeb 75% WP (2.5g/L)
precautions: Remove nearby juniper/cedar trees if possible
Apply fungicide at pink bud stage

Apple___healthy.symptoms: No disease symptoms observed. Plant appears vigorous and healthy.

Blueberry___healthy.symptoms: No disease symptoms. Plant shows normal growth and healthy foliage.
precautions: Monitor for early signs of disease

Cherry_(including_sour)___Powdery_mildew.symptoms: White powdery fungal growth on leaf surfaces. Leaves may curl, distort, or drop prematurely. Young shoots may be stunted.
Cherry_(including_sour)___Powdery_mildew.pesticide: Sulfur 80% WP (3g/L), Myclobutanil 10% WP (1g/L), or Potassium bicarbonate (5g/L)
precautions: Ensure adequate spacing for air circulation
Apply fungicide at first sign of symptoms
Water at base of plant, avoid wetting foliage

Cherry_(including_sour)___healthy.symptoms: No disease observed. Plant is in good health with normal foliage.

Corn_(maize)___Cercospora_leaf_spot Gray.symptoms: Rectangular gray-tan lesions running parallel to leaf veins. Lesions may merge causing large dead areas. Severe cases cause premature death of lower leaves.
Corn_(maize)___Cercospora_leaf_spot Gray.pesticide: Azoxystrobin 25% SC (1ml/L), Propiconazole 25% EC (1ml/L), or Trifloxystrobin 25% WG (0.5g/L)

Corn_(maize)___Common_rust_.symptoms: Small, circular to elongated brick-red pustules on both leaf surfaces. Pustules rupture releasing powdery rust-colored spores. Severe infection causes yellowing.
Corn_(maize)___Common_rust_.pesticide: Mancozeb 75% WP (2.5g/L), Zineb 75% WP (2g/L), or Propiconazole 25% EC (1ml/L)
precautions: Monitor fields from early vegetative stages
Apply fungicide if 50% of plants show symptoms before tasseling

Corn_(maize)___Northern_Leaf_Blight.symptoms: Long (1-6 inch) cigar-shaped gray-green to tan lesions on leaves. Lesions may have dark green water-soaked borders. Severe cases may affect ear husks.
Corn_(maize)___Northern_Leaf_Blight.pesticide: Azoxystrobin 25% SC (1ml/L), Propiconazole 25% EC (1ml/L), or Tebuconazole 25.9% EC (1ml/L)
precautions: Deep tillage to bury infected residue
Foliar fungicide application at early tassel

Corn_(maize)___healthy.symptoms: No disease present. Corn plant shows healthy, vigorous growth.

Grape___Black_rot.symptoms: Small yellow spots on leaves that enlarge with dark brown borders. Infected berries shrivel and turn into hard, black mummified fruit.
Grape___Black_rot.pesticide: Captan 50% WP (2g/L), Myclobutanil 10% WP (1g/L), or Mancozeb 75% WP (2.5g/L)
precautions: Begin fungicide applications at 1-inch shoot growth
Ensure good canopy management for air flow

Grape___Esca_(Black_Measles).symptoms: Tiger-stripe pattern of yellow and brown on leaves. Dark, water-soaked berries that dry up. Internal wood shows brown streaking and necrosis.
Grape___Esca_(Black_Measles).pesticide: Sodium arsenite (restricted use), Thiophanate-methyl 70% WP as wound protectant. Primarily managed culturally.
precautions: Paint pruning wounds with fungicide paste

Grape___Leaf_blight_(Isariopsis_Leaf_Spo.symptoms: Irregular dark brown spots on leaves, often surrounded by yellow halos. Spots coalesce causing large dead areas. Premature defoliation in severe cases.
Grape___Leaf_blight_(Isariopsis_Leaf_Spo.pesticide: Copper oxychloride 50% WP (3g/L), Mancozeb 75% WP (2.5g/L), or Zineb 75% WP (2g/L)

Grape___healthy.symptoms: No disease symptoms. Vine shows normal, vigorous growth.

Orange___Haunglongbing_(Citrus_greening).symptoms: Asymmetric yellowing of leaves (blotchy mottle). Fruits remain green, small, and misshapen. Bitter taste in fruit. Tree decline over several years.
Orange___Haunglongbing_(Citrus_greening).pesticide: No cure exists. Oxytetracycline HCl trunk injection may slow progression. Imidacloprid (0.5ml/L) to control psyllid vector.
precautions: Remove and destroy infected trees immediately
Control Asian citrus psyllid with insecticides

Peach___Bacterial_spot.symptoms: Small water-soaked spots on leaves that turn purple-brown with yellow halos. Fruit develops raised, corky spots. Severe leaf drop reduces yield.
Peach___Bacterial_spot.pesticide: Copper hydroxide 77% WP (3g/L), Oxytetracycline 200ppm spray, or Copper oxychloride 50% WP (3g/L)
precautions: Apply copper sprays during dormant season

Peach___healthy.symptoms: No disease observed. Peach tree in good health.
precautions: Annual pruning for shape and air flow

Pepper,_bell___Bacterial_spot.symptoms: Small water-soaked spots on leaves becoming brown with yellow halos. Raised, scab-like lesions on fruit. Severe cases cause defoliation and fruit loss.
Pepper,_bell___Bacterial_spot.pesticide: Copper hydroxide 77% WP (3g/L), Copper oxychloride 50% WP (2.5g/L), or Mancozeb 75% WP (2g/L)
precautions: Avoid working in fields when wet

Pepper,_bell___healthy.symptoms: No disease symptoms. Plant growing vigorously.

Potato___Early_blight.symptoms: Dark brown spots with concentric rings (target board pattern) on older leaves. Yellowing around spots. Tubers may show dark, corky lesions.
Potato___Early_blight.pesticide: Mancozeb 75% WP (2.5g/L), Chlorothalonil 75% WP (2g/L), or Azoxystrobin 25% SC (1ml/L)
precautions: Start fungicide spray at first symptom
Apply every 7-14 days during wet weather
Remove infected plant debris after harvest

Potato___Late_blight.symptoms: Water-soaked pale green to brown lesions on leaves, often with white mold on undersides. Rapid spread during cool, wet weather. Tubers show reddish-brown rot.
Potato___Late_blight.pesticide: Metalaxyl + Mancozeb 72% WP (2.5g/L), Cymoxanil + Mancozeb 72% WP (2.5g/L), or Dimethomorph 50% WP (1g/L)
precautions: Apply protective fungicide before disease onset
Destroy cull piles and volunteer plants

Potato___healthy.symptoms: No disease symptoms detected. Plant looks healthy.

Raspberry___healthy.symptoms: No disease found. Canes are healthy and vigorous.
precautions: Monitor for aphids and cane borers

Soybean___healthy.symptoms: No disease present. Soybean plant is healthy.
precautions: Monitor for soybean rust during humid conditions

Squash___Powdery_mildew.symptoms: White powdery coating on upper leaf surfaces. Infected leaves turn yellow and dry out. Severely affected plants have reduced fruit quality.
Squash___Powdery_mildew.pesticide: Sulfur 80% WP (3g/L), Azoxystrobin 25% SC (1ml/L), or Potassium bicarbonate (5g/L)
precautions: Apply fungicide at first sign of disease

Strawberry___Leaf_scorch.symptoms: Irregular purple to red spots on leaf surfaces, resembling scorch marks. Centers may turn gray or tan. Severe infection causes leaf death.
Strawberry___Leaf_scorch.pesticide: Captan 50% WP (2g/L), Myclobutanil 10% WP (1g/L), or Copper hydroxide 77% WP (3g/L)
precautions: Apply fungicide in spring and fall

Strawberry___healthy.symptoms: No disease symptoms. Plant is healthy with good fruit production.
precautions: Monitor for spider mites and aphids

Tomato___Bacterial_spot.symptoms: Small water-soaked spots on leaves turning dark brown with yellow halos. Fruit shows raised, scab-like spots. Defoliation in severe cases reduces yield.
Tomato___Bacterial_spot.pesticide: Copper hydroxide 77% WP (3g/L), Copper oxychloride 50% WP (2.5g/L), or Mancozeb 75% WP (2g/L)
precautions: Rotate away from tomatoes for 2 years

Tomato___Early_blight.symptoms: Dark brown concentric ring spots on older leaves. Yellow areas surrounding lesions. Stem lesions may cause collar rot in seedlings.
Tomato___Early_blight.pesticide: Mancozeb 75% WP (2.5g/L), Chlorothalonil 75% WP (2g/L), or Azoxystrobin 25% SC (1ml/L)
precautions: Apply mulch to reduce soil splash
Start fungicide early in the season
Stake plants for better air circulation

Tomato___Late_blight.symptoms: Greasy water-soaked patches on leaves turning brown-black. White mold visible on undersides in humid conditions. Fruit shows firm brown lesions.
Tomato___Late_blight.pesticide: Metalaxyl + Mancozeb 72% WP (2.5g/L), Cymoxanil + Mancozeb 72% WP (2.5g/L), or Famoxadone + Cymoxanil 22.1% SC (1ml/L)
precautions: Apply fungicide preventively during wet weather

Tomato___Leaf_Mold.symptoms: Pale green or yellow patches on upper leaf surfaces. Olive-green to gray velvety mold on undersides. Lower leaves affected first.
Tomato___Leaf_Mold.pesticide: Mancozeb 75% WP (2.5g/L), Chlorothalonil 75% WP (2g/L), or Difenoconazole 25% EC (0.5ml/L)
precautions: Remove lower leaves to improve airflow

Tomato___Septoria_leaf_spot.symptoms: Small circular spots with dark brown margins and gray-white centers on leaves. Tiny black dots (pycnidia) visible in lesion centers. Rapid defoliation.
Tomato___Septoria_leaf_spot.pesticide: Mancozeb 75% WP (2.5g/L), Chlorothalonil 75% WP (2g/L), or Azoxystrobin 25% SC (1ml/L)
precautions: Apply fungicide every 7-10 days during wet weather
Stake and prune for air circulation

Tomato___Spider_mites Two-spotted_spider.symptoms: Fine stippling or bronzing on upper leaf surfaces. Webbing on undersides of leaves. Tiny moving dots visible on leaf undersides. Leaf drop in severe cases.
Tomato___Spider_mites Two-spotted_spider.pesticide: Abamectin 1.8% EC (0.5ml/L), Spiromesifen 22.9% SC (1ml/L), or Bifenazate 43% SC (1ml/L)
precautions: Avoid dusty conditions that promote mite outbreaks
Use overhead irrigation to wash mites off leaves
Avoid broad-spectrum pesticides that kill natural enemies

Tomato___Target_Spot.symptoms: Circular brown spots with concentric rings on leaves and stems. Lesions may coalesce. Dark brown sunken spots on fruit in severe cases.
Tomato___Target_Spot.pesticide: Azoxystrobin 25% SC (1ml/L), Tebuconazole 25.9% EC (1ml/L), or Chlorothalonil 75% WP (2g/L)
precautions: Stake plants to improve air circulation
Apply mulch to reduce soil splash

Tomato___Tomato_Yellow_Leaf_Curl_Virus.symptoms: Upward curling and yellowing of young leaves. Stunted plant growth. Reduced flower and fruit set. Leaves have wrinkled, leathery texture.
Tomato___Tomato_Yellow_Leaf_Curl_Virus.pesticide: No direct cure. Control whitefly vector: Imidacloprid 17.8% SL (0.5ml/L), Thiamethoxam 25% WG (0.3g/L), or Spirotetramat 15% OD (1ml/L)
precautions: Use reflective mulches to repel whiteflies
Apply insecticides to control whitefly populations

Tomato___Tomato_mosaic_virus.symptoms: Mosaic pattern of light and dark green on leaves. Leaf distortion and curling. Stunted growth. Fruit may show uneven ripening or internal browning.
Tomato___Tomato_mosaic_virus.pesticide: No cure. Manage mechanically: disinfect tools with 10% bleach solution. Control aphid vectors with Imidacloprid 17.8% SL (0.5ml/L).

Tomato___healthy.symptoms: No disease present. Tomato plant is vigorous and healthy.
